clusters.title: Hantavirus: Ship evacuations enter second day
clusters.neutral_headline: Two more cruise ship passengers test positive for hantavirus
bullet_summary: The passengers landed in the UK on Sunday and none have reported symptoms, but they will will be monitored in hospital for 72 hours
The evacuation of passengers of all nationalities will be completed on Monday, with flights arriving from Australia and the Netherlands, Spain’s health minister has said
A French national evacuated from a luxury cruise ship linked to a deadly hantavirus outbreak tested positive for the virus, the French health minister said Monday
Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage

items.title: Hantavirus: Ship evacuations enter second day
items.standfirst: The last 24 passengers aboard the MV Hondius are set to evacuate the ship on May 11, before the vessel gets disinfected and goes back to the Netherlands. At least two passengers have tested positive since disembarking.
items.content: The last 24 passengers aboard the MV Hondius are set to evacuate the ship on May 11, before the vessel gets disinfected and goes back to the Netherlands. At least two passengers have tested positive since disembarking.

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump rejects Iran response to US peace proposal as Tehran warns of new attacks
items.standfirst: US president calls Iranian response ‘totally unacceptable’ while Tehran says it will retaliate against any new US strikes or foreign warships in strait of HormuzTrump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire fraysHezbollah started firing at Israel shortly after the US and Israel launched its war on Iran by killing the country’s former supreme leader on 28 February. Israel responded with airstrikes and launched a ground invasion of southern Lebanon.The Israeli military now occupies a strip of Lebanese land along the border. Officials claim they want to create a ‘security zone’ to protect Israel’s northern communities from Hezbollah attacks. But this has stoked fears of a long-term occupation. Continue reading...
items.content: US president calls Iranian response ‘totally unacceptable’ while Tehran says it will retaliate against any new US strikes or foreign warships in strait of HormuzTrump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire fraysHezbollah started firing at Israel shortly after the US and Israel launched its war on Iran by killing the country’s former supreme leader on 28 February. Israel responded with airstrikes and launched a ground invasion of southern Lebanon.The Israeli military now occupies a strip of Lebanese land along the border. Officials claim they want to create a ‘security zone’ to protect Israel’s northern communities from Hezbollah attacks. But this has stoked fears of a long-term occupation. Continue reading...

items.title: 17 Americans from hantavirus cruise ship arrive in U.S. for quarantine
items.standfirst: An American on the repatriation flight began showing symptoms of hantavirus and another "tested mildly PCR positive for the Andes virus," the Department of Health and Human Services says.
items.content: An American on the repatriation flight began showing symptoms of hantavirus and another "tested mildly PCR positive for the Andes virus," the Department of Health and Human Services says.

items.standfirst: The passengers landed in the UK on Sunday and none have reported symptoms, but they will will be monitored in hospital for 72 hours.
items.content: The passengers landed in the UK on Sunday and none have reported symptoms, but they will will be monitored in hospital for 72 hours.

items.title: Crude oil prices rise as Trump calls Iran's offer 'totally unacceptable'
items.standfirst: Benchmark crude oil prices rose on Monday as US President Donald Trump rejected Iran's latest counterproposal to find an end to the war in the Middle East. The continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz is leaving its mark on economies around the world, with India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi calling for reduced fuel use and Chinese inflation gauges ticking up higher than expected. Also in this edition: French President Emmanuel Macron announces more than €1 billion in investment in Kenya.
items.content: Benchmark crude oil prices rose on Monday as US President Donald Trump rejected Iran's latest counterproposal to find an end to the war in the Middle East. The continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz is leaving its mark on economies around the world, with India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi calling for reduced fuel use and Chinese inflation gauges ticking up higher than expected. Also in this edition: French President Emmanuel Macron announces more than €1 billion in investment in Kenya.

items.title: Evacuated US and French MV Hondius passengers test positive for hantavirus
items.standfirst: French woman taken to Paris in serious condition while American flown to Nebraska is asymptomatic, say officialsEurope live – latest updatesA French woman and an American national evacuated from the cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak have tested positive for the virus, as the complex operation to repatriate those onboard continued on Monday.The French woman was one of five French passengers who disembarked from the ship in Tenerife on Sunday before being flown to a hospital in Paris. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers enter isolation facility after evacuation to UK
items.standfirst: Twenty-two people from MV Hondius cruise spend first day isolating in self-contained flats in MerseysideWhat is hantavirus?Passengers evacuated to the UK from a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak are spending their first day at an isolation facility after being repatriated from Tenerife.A chartered Titan Airways flight transported the MV Hondius passengers from the Canary Islands to Manchester airport on Sunday evening. The evacuation of passengers of all nationalities will be completed on Monday, with flights arriving from Australia and the Netherlands, Spain’s health minister has said. Continue reading...
items.content: Twenty-two people from MV Hondius cruise spend first day isolating in self-contained flats in MerseysideWhat is hantavirus?Passengers evacuated to the UK from a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak are spending their first day at an isolation facility after being repatriated from Tenerife.A chartered Titan Airways flight transported the MV Hondius passengers from the Canary Islands to Manchester airport on Sunday evening. The evacuation of passengers of all nationalities will be completed on Monday, with flights arriving from Australia and the Netherlands, Spain’s health minister has said. Continue reading...

items.title: French evacuee from hantavirus-hit ship tests positive, health minister says
items.standfirst: A French national evacuated from a luxury cruise ship linked to a deadly hantavirus outbreak tested positive for the virus, the French health minister said Monday. Four other French passengers from the ship tested negative, but will be re-tested, the minister added.
items.content: A French national evacuated from a luxury cruise ship linked to a deadly hantavirus outbreak tested positive for the virus, the French health minister said Monday. Four other French passengers from the ship tested negative, but will be re-tested, the minister added.

items.title: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship heading back home

items.title: Trump calls Iran's response to US peace proposal 'totally unacceptable'
items.standfirst: Iran rejected the latest US peace proposal on Sunday, prompting a swift dismissal from President Donald Trump, who called the response “TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE!” on social media. The exchange marks another setback in efforts to ease tensions in the Persian Gulf, where disrupted shipping and rising energy prices continue to fuel global concern.
items.content: Iran rejected the latest US peace proposal on Sunday, prompting a swift dismissal from President Donald Trump, who called the response “TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE!” on social media. The exchange marks another setback in efforts to ease tensions in the Persian Gulf, where disrupted shipping and rising energy prices continue to fuel global concern.

items.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire frays
items.standfirst: US president expresses ire at Tehran’s reported demands, as drones strike Gulf nations and Israel warns war ‘not over’Donald Trump has rejected an Iranian response to a US peace proposal as “totally unacceptable”, on a day the month-old ceasefire showed signs of fraying as drone strikes were reported around the region and Benjamin Netanyahu warned the war was “not over”.The Iranian counter-proposal was passed to Washington through Pakistani mediators. Continue reading...
items.content: US president expresses ire at Tehran’s reported demands, as drones strike Gulf nations and Israel warns war ‘not over’Donald Trump has rejected an Iranian response to a US peace proposal as “totally unacceptable”, on a day the month-old ceasefire showed signs of fraying as drone strikes were reported around the region and Benjamin Netanyahu warned the war was “not over”.The Iranian counter-proposal was passed to Washington through Pakistani mediators. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to U.S. peace proposal ‘totally unacceptable’
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to a U.S. proposal to bring an end to the war in the Middle East, calling the message “totally unacceptable” on Truth Social.“I have just read the response from Iran’s so-called ‘Representatives,’” Trump posted Sunday afternoon.
items.content: President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to a U.S. proposal to bring an end to the war in the Middle East, calling the message “totally unacceptable” on Truth Social.“I have just read the response from Iran’s so-called ‘Representatives,’” Trump posted Sunday afternoon.

clusters.title: Remains of US soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
clusters.neutral_headline: Body of missing US soldier recovered off coast of Morocco
bullet_summary: soldier who went missing during military drills in Morocco were recovered in the Atlantic Ocean, the army said
Army announced on Sunday the remains of one of the two missing American soldiers in Morocco has been recovered
Moroccan searchers found the remains on Saturday in the water within a mile (1
6km) of where the soldier went missing on 2 May, the army said in a statement

items.title: Remains of US soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
items.standfirst: The remains of a U.S. soldier who went missing during military drills in Morocco were recovered in the Atlantic Ocean, the army said. Military teams are searching for a second missing soldier.
items.content: The remains of a U.S. soldier who went missing during military drills in Morocco were recovered in the Atlantic Ocean, the army said. Military teams are searching for a second missing soldier.(Image credit: Mosa'ab Elshamy)

items.content: The Moroccan military recovered the body of one of two U.S. soldiers who went missing on May 2, the U.S. Army said.

items.title: Body of missing US soldier recovered off coast of Morocco
items.standfirst: 1st Lt Kendrick Lamont Key Jr was participating in largest military exercise among US, Nato allies and African nationsA search team recovered the body of a US soldier who went missing near a cliff during a training exercise in Cap Draa, Morocco, the US Army said on Sunday.Moroccan searchers found the remains on Saturday in the water within a mile (1.6km) of where the soldier went missing on 2 May, the army said in a statement. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Ailing Iranian Nobel laureate given bail and hospital transfer
clusters.neutral_headline: Ailing Iranian Nobel laureate given bail and hospital transfer
bullet_summary: Nobel Peace laureate and activist Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to a Tehran hospital more than a week after collapsing in prison, her foundation said Sunday
The activist, jailed for campaigning against Iran's clerical system, reportedly suffered two suspected heart attacks in custody

items.title: Ailing Iranian Nobel laureate given bail and hospital transfer
items.standfirst: Iranian authorities granted human rights activist Narges Mohammadi bail amid growing concern over her poor health in prison.
items.content: Iranian authorities granted human rights activist Narges Mohammadi bail amid growing concern over her poor health in prison.

items.title: Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi transferred to a Tehran hospital, her foundation says
items.standfirst: Nobel Peace laureate and activist Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to a Tehran hospital more than a week after collapsing in prison, her foundation said Sunday.
items.content: Nobel Peace laureate and activist Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to a Tehran hospital more than a week after collapsing in prison, her foundation said Sunday.(Image credit: Narges Foundation Archive)

items.title: Iran releases Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi on bail amid health fears
items.standfirst: Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi has been released on bail and transferred to Tehran for urgent medical treatment after supporters warned she was at risk of dying in prison. The activist, jailed for campaigning against Iran's clerical system, reportedly suffered two suspected heart attacks in custody.
items.content: Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi has been released on bail and transferred to Tehran for urgent medical treatment after supporters warned she was at risk of dying in prison. The activist, jailed for campaigning against Iran's clerical system, reportedly suffered two suspected heart attacks in custody.

items.standfirst: Influential former prime minister released on parole after spending about eight months behind bars.
items.content: Influential former prime minister released on parole after spending about eight months behind bars.

items.title: Former Thai PM Thaksin Shinawatra released from prison on parole
items.standfirst: Former Thai prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra was released from prison on parole on Monday after serving eight months of a reduced sentence tied to corruption charges. His return to public life marks another chapter in a political saga that has dominated and deeply divided Thailand for nearly two decades.
items.content: Former Thai prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra was released from prison on parole on Monday after serving eight months of a reduced sentence tied to corruption charges. His return to public life marks another chapter in a political saga that has dominated and deeply divided Thailand for nearly two decades.

items.standfirst: Barcelona secure 29th Spanish league title after beating Madrid, with goals from Marcus Rashford and Ferran Torres.
items.content: Barcelona secure 29th Spanish league title after beating Madrid, with goals from Marcus Rashford and Ferran Torres.

items.title: Barcelona clinch La Liga title with Clasico win over Real Madrid
items.standfirst: Barcelona secured their 29th Spanish league title with a 2-0 Clasico victory over Real Madrid at Camp Nou on Sunday. Marcus Rashford and Ferran Torres struck in the first half as Hansi Flick's side sealed consecutive La Liga crowns, leaving Madrid facing a second straight trophyless season.
items.content: Barcelona secured their 29th Spanish league title with a 2-0 Clasico victory over Real Madrid at Camp Nou on Sunday. Marcus Rashford and Ferran Torres struck in the first half as Hansi Flick's side sealed consecutive La Liga crowns, leaving Madrid facing a second straight trophyless season.

items.title: Middle East live: Iran war cannot end before uranium is ‘taken out’, Netanyahu says
items.standfirst: Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium must be "taken out" before the US-Israeli war can be considered over, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told a US broadcaster after President Donald Trump said Tehran's latest proposal for ending the conflict was "totally unacceptable". Iran on Monday said its terms were "legitimate" and "generous". Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium must be "taken out" before the US-Israeli war can be considered over, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told a US broadcaster after President Donald Trump said Tehran's latest proposal for ending the conflict was "totally unacceptable". Iran on Monday said its terms were "legitimate" and "generous". Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
